message.content: How do I center a div in CSS? I want it horizontally and vertically centered on the page.
Nice! What about using CSS Grid instead? Does that work for centering too?
Thanks! One more question - I'm building a login form. How do I add a password field that hides the text as the user types?

Use the HTML input type="password":

```html
<form>
  <label for="pwd">Password:</label>
  <input type="password" id="pwd" name="password" required>
  <button type="submit">Log In</button>
</form>
```

The `type="password"` attribute automatically masks the input as the user types. Never store or transmit passwords in plain text - always hash them server-side.
Good to know. Speaking of passwords, how do I properly hash passwords in Python on the backend?

Use the `bcrypt` library. It's the standard for password hashing:

```python
import bcrypt

# Hashing
password = "user_password"
hashed = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt())

# Verifying
is_correct = bcrypt.checkpw(password.encode(), hashed)
```

Don't implement your own hashing algorithm. `bcrypt` automatically handles salt generation and is resistant to brute force attacks. Never store plain text passwords.
